title: For the Marriage That Has Lost Its Spark
scripture_text: Love bears all things, believes all things, hopes all things, endures all things.
reflection: The spark is not the marriage. The marriage is what you build by choosing to show up, especially when the spark has wandered off.
body:
You can live with someone for ten, twenty, thirty years and forget, in the middle of an ordinary Tuesday, why you chose them. This is not a crisis. It is a feature of long love.

The early spark was never going to last. It could not. It was a spark, not a fire. The fire is what you are building now, slowly, by getting through the Tuesday.

Tell them one specific thing you appreciate about them. Tonight. Not "I love you" — too easy. Something specific. "I noticed you took out the trash without being asked." "Thank you for the way you handled that with the kids."

The marriage does not need a spark. It needs a thousand small attentions. The spark comes back on its own, when you stop looking for it.
prayer: Lord, help me love this person well today. Not dramatically. Just well. Amen.
